programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Pour ou contre les Airbnb permis seulement l'été?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Jean-Michel Dufaux, Maude Goyer, Jacinthe-Eve Arel et notre invitée l'animatrice Elisabeth Crête.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Pour ou contre boycotter les produits américains?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Victor Henriquez, Jacinthe-Eve Arel, Valérie Beaudoin et notre invité l'entrepreneur Nicolas Duvernois.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Qui a gagné hier : le Canada ou Donald Trump?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Valérie Beaudoin, Victor Henriquez, Déborah Cherenfant et Justine McIntyre.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Doit-on craindre une montée de l'homophobie et de la transphobie chez nous?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Jacinthe-Eve Arel, PL Cloutier, Justine McIntyre et notre invitée l'influenceuse trans Gabrielle Marion.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Doit-on mettre sur pause les hymnes nationaux dans le sport le temps des menaces tarifaires?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Meeker Guerrier, Antonine Yaccarini, Catherine Beauchamp et notre invité l'analyste sports et affaires Ray Lalonde.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Pour ou contre suspendre les voyages scolaires aux États-Unis?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Jean-Michel Dufaux, Catherine Beauchamp, Maude Goyer et Meeker Guerrier.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Pour ou contre des amendes pour les discussions sur haut-parleur dans les transports publics?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Maude Goyer, Jacinthe-Eve Arel, PL Cloutier et notre invitée l'animatrice radio Elisabeth Crête.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Devrait-on ressusciter les projets d'oléoduc comme Énergie Est et GNL Québec?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Victor Henriquez, Justine McIntyre, Maude Goyer et Jacinthe-Eve Arel.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Pour ou contre les applications de rencontre?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Meeker Guerrier, PL Cloutier, Déborah Cherenfant et notre invité Victor Henriquez.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Les employeurs devraient-ils donner des congés « tempête » à leurs employés?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Victor Henriquez, Catherine Beauchamp, PL Cloutier et Déborah Cherenfant.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Devrait-on rendre obligatoire les cours de conduite hivernale?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Meeker Guerrier, Jacinthe-Eve Arel, Raphaël Melançon et notre invité André Durocher de CAA-Québec.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Donald Trump peut-il vraiment mettre fin à la guerre en Ukraine?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Victor Henriquez, Antonine Yaccarini, Valérie Beaudoin et l'avocat en droit constitutionnel Me Frédéric Bérard.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Pour ou contre limiter la durée des grèves et « lock-out »?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Meeker Guerrier, Jacinthe-Eve Arel, l'animatrice Elisabeth Crête et l'avocat en droit constitutionnel Me Frédéric Bérard.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Mark Carney serait-il la bonne personne pour faire face à la guerre tarifaire avec les États-Unis?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Victor Henriquez, Jacinthe-Eve Arel, Maude Goyer et Déborah Cherenfant.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Fiasco SAAQclic : devrait-il y avoir une enquête publique?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Meeker Guerrier, Victor Henriquez, Déborah Cherenfant et notre invité l'avocat en droit constitutionnel Me Frédéric Bérard.

programme.title: His Girl Friday
programme.desc: A newspaper editor uses every trick in the book to stop his ex- wife from remarrying and moving.

programme.title: Only Angels Have Wings
programme.desc: Grant, the head of a broken-down air freight company, sends courageous pilots over the Andes Mountains.

programme.title: Twentieth Century
programme.desc: A comedy based on the Broadway hit where a maniacal Broadway director transforms a shopgirl into a success.

programme.title: A Dandy In Aspic
programme.desc: British Intelligence orders its operatives to eliminate a Soviet Spy not realizing he's one of their own men.

programme.title: Charade
programme.desc: Before discovering her husband has been murdered in Paris, Regina Lampert falls for the charming Peter Joshua. Starring legendary screen icons Audrey Hepburn and Cary Grant.

programme.title: A Map of the World
programme.desc: A school nurse (Sigourney Weaver) faces the scorn of her community after an accident on her property.

programme.title: The Way
programme.desc: Martin Sheen ("The Departed") stars as a father who, upon recovering the body of his estranged son who died traveling the "El Camino de Santiago," decides to take the pilgrimage himself.

programme.title: Rush of Fear
programme.desc: Rosanna Arquette and Chris Potter star as couple Alex and Jack, who are away on a romantic weekend to try to salvage their broken marriage. Their trip turns into a nightmare when Jack is kidnapped.

programme.title: Transsiberian
programme.desc: On their way home from China, married Christian missionaries Roy (Woody Harrelson) and Jessie (Emily Mortimer) take a train from Beijing to Moscow. On board, they befriend Carlos (Eduardo Noriega) and Abby (Kate Mara). When Roy does not return in time at one stop, Jessie waits for him to catch up at the next station. Carlos and Abby stay with her, insisting the town is unsafe, however Carlos' attempts to "befriend" Jessie radically change the dynamic of the trip.

programme.title: The Patriot
programme.desc: A rural Montana community is threatened when the FBI tries to arrest members of a radical militia group. The militia retaliates by releasing a killer virus into the air, sickening most of the local residents -- but not Dr. Wesley McClaren (Steven Seagal) or his daughter, Holly (Gailard Sartain). It seems that Holly is immune to the virus. As McClaren races to find a cure, militia leader Floyd Chisolm (L.Q. Jones) attempts to kidnap Holly so that his group can make an antitoxin.

programme.title: Lou's Big Gamble

But first, Ronnie and Froy must wrangle a wrestling ring from an lucha libre school, and Carlos and Matt have to break up a Botox party when they go after a car owned by a upper class housewife. Then on their first repo in Vegas, a live tiger stands between the team and a truck, and it seems like Vegas is more than they can handle. If they complete their mission, the payout could be enormous, but will they be able to resist the temptations of ...

programme.desc: The team returns from a successful mission in Vegas, but there's trouble brewing within the crew's ranks. Ronnie and Carlos repo from a self-defense instructor who lets his students do his dirty work, Matt and Froy confront a delinquent delivery man steeling flowers from graves, and Lou and Carlos are left defenseless against gang members with blowtorches. Can Lou still keep his cool in the high pressure repo business, or will there be repercussions for Matt and Froy that no one saw coming?

programme.title: Daddy's Little Girl

Lou and Sonia head to a dark alley to take a van away from a pair of libidinous teens with a dark secret, Sonia and Froy come face to face with the world's most ornery baker and come away a little worse for wear, and Matt and Froy get in way over their heads when they attempt a repo at a high-octane truck racing track.

programme.title: Karen Morgan
programme.desc: A former trial attorney, she moved from the courtroom to comedy after being selected as a Finalist on Nickelodeon TV's Search for the Funniest Mom in America.

programme.title: Chris Cope
programme.desc: Chris Cope, a Florida transplant now living in LA. The city has been good to Chris in just 4 short years he's appeared on Jimmy Kimmel Live, Tosh.O, Laughs on Fox. He's acted in feature films and also appeared on the hit ABC TV Show "Grey's Anatomy".

programme.title: Johnny Beehner
programme.desc: Johnny Beehner puts on a funny show. He's got everything you want from a comic: He's silly, he's likeable, and he's fun. With a background in improvisational theater including training at Chicago's prestigious Second City, as well as the Upright Citizens Brigade in Los Angeles, Johnny brings a unique style to the stage. Beehner made his network television debut in January of 2015 as one of the last comedians to appear on the Late Show with David Letterman. He has made numerous appearances on FOX "Laughs" television show, and regularly appears on the nationally syndicated Bob & Tom Show. His comedy can be heard regularly on XM/ Sirius Satellite radio. Other notable and accomplishments of Johnny's include winning First place in Comcast's 2014 season of "Trial By Laughter," placing third in the 2014 “Laughing Skull Comedy Festival” of Atlanta, and making it to the finals in the 2012 “Great American Comedy Festival”. He was named one of Ricky Gervais' Top 5 picks in the "Just Sayin” stand up contest. Johnny was recently named one of 2015's Top10, "Hot Comics to Watch" by his wife. She says that he would have a shot at the #1 spot if he would “work just a little bit harder”. He's a married man, a new dad, and his current life goal is to win just one argument with his beautiful wife.

programme.title: Robert Mac
programme.desc: Robert Mac returns to the Dry Bar stage with less smartness, less subtleness, and even more silliness.

programme.title: Mike Paramore
programme.desc: Mike Paramore is the guy you fight to sit next to in a movie theatre, on a long car ride, or during a boring lecture. His natural ability to infuse everyday situations with energetic, uplifting humor, combined with a natural ability to put you at ease is key to his personal brand of comedy. His smooth delivery and? powerful punchlines make him a force in stand-up comedy, which is very apparent in his debut album "The Things We Tell Ourselves", available now on iTunes and Amazon. Mike has opened for comedians like Damon Wayans, Cash Cab's Ben Baily, Jon Lovitz, and Saturday Night Live's Jay Pharoah.

programme.title: DC Benny
programme.desc: Benny lives in Brooklyn where he fiddles around with old Land Rovers when he is supposed to be helping his wife run her natural skincare business. He has a dog named Grits and two cats who prefer that he not share their personal information in this bio.

programme.title: Michael Joiner
programme.desc: Michaelís brand of comedy is, in a word, familiar. Not in the sense that youíve heard it before, but more so that he knows you and you know him. He speaks about the things you are thinking in a way that sounds so much like your inner voice it is astounding. His effortless delivery will resonate with you. Michael is the 1st Place winner of the Hollywood Improv's "FUNNIEST COMIC IN L.A." 2008, and 2010 Christian Music Hall of Fame "COMEDIAN OF THE YEAR" nominee, Joiner has attracted wide praise for his routines that blend a sarcastic edginess with a devotion to clean humor. Joiner has headlined at The LA AREA Improvs, The Funny Bone, The Ice House and other comedy clubs across the country while also performing at colleges and sold out Clean Comedy Concerts nationwide. He's also written for "The Tonight Show With Jay Leno." He has been performing nationwide for over 25 years and is rated one of the nation's funniest Clean Stand-up Comedians. The TV Guide Network calls Michael "One of the funniest, most original, stand up Comedians working today!" In 2006, Michael was one out of only four Comedians chosen worldwide to star in the top selling DVD, "The Clean Stand Up Comedy Tour." Unlike many Comedians who stick to their rehearsed script, Michael's stand up comedy show includes some of the most clever, hilarious improv and audience interaction you will ever experience.

programme.title: Richard Sarvate
programme.desc: Richard Sarvate quit his ten year programming career in Silicon Valley to be a stand up comedian. Oops! Now he spends his nights poking fun at tech jobs.

programme.title: Josh Novey
programme.desc: Iraq War veteran Josh Novey takes the stage with an easy-going presence and jokes about Waffle House, kindly threatening his audience, and tickling grizzly bears.

programme.title: Tyler Boeh
programme.desc: Who goes from being a mild-mannered nationally recognized college a Capella group vocal percussionist to becoming a high-energy, intelligent, physical, and downright silly beatboxing comedian? None other than Comedian Tyler Boeh, who shifted his focus from singing a Capella (a word he describes as Latin for “Without Girlfriend”) when he won a comedy contest in Portland Oregon. After winning and placing in the finals of numerous Comedy Competitions from San Francisco to Seattle to Boston to The World Series of Comedy, Boeh not only launched a great career in comedy including television appearances, colleges, cruise ships and headlining clubs, he also found a girlfriend. Tyler's comedy CD was chosen by LaughSpin Magazine as the Best Comedy Album of 2012 and you may have seen him on such shows as Celebrity Name Game, Moms Rule, Nesn Comedy All Stars, or heard him on SIRIUS radio. Don't miss a chance to see Tyler live.

programme.title: Helene Angley: Queen Size
programme.desc: Helene brings honesty to the stage that helps everyone relax-- and laugh at the joys and pains of aging, being a married mother of two and all that the generation gap brings!

programme.sub-title: Baby's First Sounds: Discoveries for Little Ears
programme.desc: Perhaps the sweetest sounds you'll ever hear are the babbles and coos that one day become ma-ma, da-da and I love you. Besides being music to your ears, these baby steps of language change everything as your little one discovers an exciting, powerful new way of relating to the world! Baby's First Sounds invites both of you to share these memorable moments: those precious ''firsts'' that come around once in your baby's lifetime.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.sub-title: Let's Explore: Swordfish, Lobsters, Beluga Whales, and Flamingos
programme.desc: Join the Ocean Explorers crew and learn about swordfish, lobsters, beluga whales, and flamingos! Enjoy real-life footage of these majestic creatures. Discover sorting, the concepts of pairs, imitation, and opposites . Sing and dance along to The Ring Toss Song, Where Is The Pair?, Beluga Simon Says, and The Stand on One Leg Song.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.sub-title: Baby Einstein: Let's Learn about Dogs, Cats and More
programme.desc: Meet Cats, Dogs, Birds, Bugs and Woodland Creatures! Learn simple words through narration and repetition. Meet all sorts of creatures from domestic kittens to jungle cats, puppies, big and small birds, butterflies, lady bugs, otters and deer!! See whimsical toys and puppets.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.sub-title: On the Go: Riding, Sailing and Soaring
programme.desc: As little ones become increasingly mobile, their fascination with all things that move intensifies too. On the Go: Riding, Sailing and Soaring exposes babies to the different ways people travel -- on land, by sea, and in the air -- while it presents them with captivating real-world images, funny puppet shows, and beautiful classical music by Beethoven, Haydn, Mozart and Rossini.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Get moving with Baby Einstein! Real, live-action footage and classical melodies engage young viewers as they discover simple words related to movement. Enjoy encouraging melodies and live-action footage of babies and animals as you learn and move alongside your favorite Baby Einstein friends. Dance along as you watch real, live action footage of animals, children, and toys.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Build some memories this Summer with your favorite handyman and his handy friends!

programme.title: Race to the Finish
programme.desc: Mayor Bentley announces that Sunflower Valley is to stage its very first athletics tournament. A team from Sunflower Valley is to compete against a team of visiting athletes. Plus there is to be a special machine event!

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Bob The Photographer / Mr Bentley's Trains
programme.desc: Bob goes to Farmer Pickles farm to take some photographs. / Mrs Bentley asks Bob to build a new model railway.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: The Mayor asks Bob to build a Pets Corner in a local park. / A burst pipe outside Mr Sabatini's needs fixing.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob, Wendy, Roley and Dizzy are finishing a new Bus Stop. / Mrs Potts's fence is behind schedule.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Mr Sabatini's Smashing Day / Roley To The Rescue
programme.desc: Bob is rendering a wall for Mr Sabatini. / After a stormy night Wendy has to mend a chimney pot at Mrs Potts'.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Wendy's Removal Service / Mr Beasley's DIY Disaster
programme.desc: Bob is carrying out a loft conversion at a new home. / Bob is building a lean-to for Mr Beasley's allotment.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob and the team build a special eco-home. / The team are building Sunflower Valley's first geodesic dome.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Spud's Big Splash / Spud The Musketeer
programme.desc: Bob is building a water feature in Mrs Potts' garden. / Wendy is making a floral display for the town square.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Build some memories this Summer with your favorite handyman and his handy friends!

programme.title: Sky's the Limit
programme.desc: When a storm causes a giant boulder to cut off Berry Bitty City's water supply, Strawberry and the girls face the possibility of having to move away from their homes. With the help of Mr. Longface Caterpillar, the girls set out on an adventure to save their town.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry Shortcake is planning a first birthday party for her sister Apple Dumpling, so she sets off on an adventure to find the perfect party supplies (juice, cookies, a cake and party hats) and makes five new friends (Orange Blossom, Angel Cake, Ginger Snap, Honey Pie Pony and Huckleberry) along the way

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: With time running out before the Grand Opening celebration for the new Marketplace, Strawberry's friends get the idea to invite her favorite singer, superstar CHERRY JAM, to be the guest singer. The girls are blown away when their berryjuice cell phone gets a text message that Cherry accepts their invitation! Strawberry is touched by their surprise, but reminds them that now the town and Marketplace really have to shine!

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Cherry Jam is so enjoying staying in Berry Bitty City, that she calls her managers in Big Bitty City and informs them that she is going to stay for a much needed vacation! Strawberry and the girls are thrilled that she will be with them for a little longer, and they do all they can to make their guest comfortable. But Cherry doesn't want to be treated like a guest she wants to fit in and contribute to the community.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: When the girls discover that Apple's groove booties can teach the pups to dance, Plum gets an idea for a Doggie Dance Show. Wanting to impress them, Huck offers to have TomTom join the show... despite the pup's obvious dislike of performing in public. It's up to Strawberry to help Huck see that he should not force TomTom into something he just doesn't want to do.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: When Sour wishes she could have a birthday all her very own, her twin sister Sweet grants her wish by forgoing celebrating her birthday. But what begins as a day that Sour has always dreamed of quickly becomes a disappointment when she misses her twin. With Strawberry's help, Sour gives her birthday back to Sweet with a special surprise.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: When a simple tune Cherry composes goes viral on the Berryweb, Cherry quickly gets fed up with everyone listening to this song, everywhere, all the time. She goes to comic lengths to get some peace so she can compose the song she's suppose to write for the Queen of Berryvania.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Berry Bitty City is holding its first city-wide talent show! Hearing that it's the music students of superstar Cherry Jam, a talent scout from Big Bitty City plans to show up. Strawberry and her friends excitedly anticipate her arrival.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry Shortcake is planning a first birthday party for her sister Apple Dumpling, so she sets off on an adventure to find the perfect party supplies.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry and Orange are dangling their legs in the stream when something tickles Orange's foot. She is sent squealing from the water, certain an eel is after her. Strawberry good-naturedly chuckles at her antics. But Orange gets her dander up and wades into the water to face her "attacker." When she looks down into the water, gazing up at her is the cutest, most adorable, puffy-cheeked fish she's ever seen. It's love at first sight. Orange has to take him home.

programme.title: HappyKids
programme.desc: HappyKids

programme.title: HappyKids
programme.desc: HappyKids

programme.title: Blippi's Adventure and Discovery Tour
programme.desc: Join Blippi as he embarks on an exciting Adventure and Discovery Tour! Explore the fascinating jobs at Wimbledon, enjoy a fun-filled game of Mini Golf, and cheer on your favorite team at Liverpool FC. Watch Blippi get hands-on with recycling in Smash My Trash, and meet Stanley the Dinosaur for a roaring good time. Each adventure is packed with fun, learning, and new discoveries, making every moment with Blippi an unforgettable experience for kids.

programme.title: Blippi's Learning and Exploration Journey
programme.desc: Set off on a Learning and Exploration Journey with Blippi as he dives into the world of construction vehicles, exploring bulldozers and excavators up close! Discover the wonders of a Children's Museum and learn through play at the Cayton Children's Museum. Get a splash of color and knowledge as Blippi teaches kids about plants and colors in a fun and engaging way. These educational adventures are perfect for curious young minds, making learning exciting and interactive with Blippi.

programme.title: No Blueberry Is an Island
programme.desc: With the summer almost over, Blueberry overhears Strawberry talking about a wonderful tropical island and believes her friend is going to surprise them all with a much needed vacation! Blueberry makes the other girls promise to keep the secret until Strawberry reveals the big surprise. Of course, even though they've promised to keep the secret, they can't help but try and suss out what the island's like from every little thing Strawberry does, so they can prepare accordingly—they see Strawberry wearing a diving mask and snorkel in the cafe, and race to the lake to practice scuba diving, not knowing she was only slicing onions.

programme.title: Where the Berry Breeze Blows
programme.desc: It's the last week of summer and Berry Bitty City is hopping with activity as the girls prepare for their tropical island vacation at Berry Breezy Bay Resort. But just as they're about to leave, bad news: the resort is closed for the season due to berry breezy weather. The girls are disappointed, but Orange isn't giving up. She says if they can't go to the tropical island, maybe they can bring the tropical island here! She insists they can build a copy of “Berry Breezy Bay” resort on Berry Bitty Lake, a duplicate down to the last detail.

programme.title: HappyKids
programme.desc: HappyKids

programme.title: I Want Cake!
programme.desc: Sid and his friends discover that only eating cake would make their bodies feel terrible. Then they explore and learn that the best way to stay healthy is by eating nutritious food.

programme.title: The Big Sneeze
programme.desc: Sid and his friends learn that germs are so small that you can't see them. And they discover that a great way to stay healthy and keep germs away is by washing their hands with soap and water!

programme.title: Tarzan and Jane
programme.desc: Baby Tarzan is rescued from a plane crash by the gorilla, Kala, and brought to a Shaman who treats Tarzan's injuries with a mix of native medicines and experimental science - saving Tarzan's life and giving him supernatural animal powers in the process. Tarzan is returned to Kala and remains in the jungle, defending animals from poachers, hunters and other threats. As a teenager, Tarzan is discovered by an expedition team from London, paid for by his billionaire grandfather. It is during this expedition where Tarzan meets Jane, and they become fast friends. Later, a series of mishaps takes him to London, reuniting Tarzan not only with his long lost family, but Jane as well. It is here where Tarzan and Jane learn to navigate the “urban jungle” of London, and uncover a dastardly plot to steal and sell exotic animals to the highest bidder! It is up to our heroes to figure out who is behind this sinister deed, and return the animals to their rightful homes in the jungle.

programme.title: Blippi's Spooky Spell Halloween
programme.desc: With a guidebook of Spooky Halloween Spells, Blippi investigates costumes, treats, and the art of pumpkin carving.

programme.title: The Berry Best Vacation
programme.desc: With only a few days of summer left, the girls are about to enjoy their first vacation in their newly-created, tropical beach resort at Berry Bitty Lake, when who should descend upon them but Mavis Maraschino, the comically over-the-top “Entertainment Tonight”-style show host/producer from Episode 206. Mavis has dumped her focus on fashions in favor of an even glitzier show called Lifestyles of the Berry Big Bitty Stars, and she wants her first segment to be about how pop superstar Cherry Jam spends her vacation. Trying to accommodate Mavis and continue their vacation doesn't work—every time the girls try to have fun together, Mavis pops up, poking a microphone in Cherry's face, insisting on treating her as if she were a Lady Gaga-type.

programme.title: The Berry Long Winter
programme.desc: On a beautiful winter's night, Blueberry reads the Baby Berrykins a story of what happened one winter in Berry Bitty City—a winter that almost wasn't. Just as everyone is ready for winter fun and fashions, a sudden warming melts the snow and ruins their plans. Improvising with snowmen made of shaved ice and cotton puffs, Strawberry and her friends play “Capture the Snowman's Carrot Nose.” The Berrykins want to join in the fun, so the girls play against them, with Strawberry refereeing. But it turns out the Berrykins' version of the game has different rules: they zoom around on little hang gliders made of leaves!

programme.title: Contact
programme.desc: Yusaku Fuiki, has become a celebrity in the aftermath of battling Hanoi. He continues to investigate the mysterious program he dubs "Ai." Meanwhile, SOL Technologies begins to look deeper into the identity of Playmaker.

programme.title: Pain and Gain
programme.desc: Go Onizuka, a popular duelist on Link VRAINS, accepts an offer from SOL to duel Playmaker. He wants to prove himself and he sees Playmaker as a powerful opponent. He lures Yusaku into a trap and a Speed Duel begins.

programme.title: Counting With Paula
programme.sub-title: Jungle Boy's Birthday Surprise / Jungle Race
programme.desc: It's Jungle Boy's birthday! The children and their jungle friends plan a surprise party.

programme.title: Counting With Paula
programme.sub-title: Redbeard's Best Friend / Action Team Awesome (ATA Intro)
programme.desc: The children help Captain Redbeard to get back his best friend, Parrot.

programme.title: Counting With Paula
programme.desc: Toby has a terrible fall and his brothers feel responsible. They try to make up for it.

programme.title: Counting With Paula
programme.sub-title: The Girl in Space / Alien Dog Show Competition
programme.desc: The children travel to Outer Space to meet Mr. Astronaut's daughter for the first time.

programme.title: ZooMoo
programme.desc: Test your animal knowledge on ZooMoo's Got Brains -- A game show like no other!

programme.title: ZooMoo
programme.sub-title: Over The Rainbow / Under The Sea
programme.desc: Test your animal knowledge on ZooMoo's Got Brains -- A game show like no other!

programme.title: Billy Bunny's Animal Songs
programme.desc: Sing along with Billy Bunny as he learns lots of new songs before dinnertime!

programme.title: Counting With Paula
programme.sub-title: Junior Astronaut Apprenticeship / Saving Captain Redbeard's Friend
programme.desc: Mr. Astronaut trains the children to be Junior Astronauts.

programme.title: Counting With Paula
programme.sub-title: Cosmo's Diet / Garden Gnomes VS City Gnomes
programme.desc: The children feed Cosmo and discover more of his hidden abilities. What else can Astrid's alien space dog do?

programme.title: Counting With Paula
programme.sub-title: Talking Animals / Fixer on the Roof
programme.desc: The children are tasked to take care of the farm animals while Mr. Farmer is away.

programme.title: Counting With Paula
programme.sub-title: Prince Aamil's Pet Bird / ''X' Marks the Spot
programme.desc: Prince Aamil has a new pet bird, but there's something wrong with it. It can't fly!

programme.title: Counting With Paula
programme.sub-title: The New Traffic Officer / The Greatest Show on Treasure Island
programme.desc: The children help Mr. Banana Tree with his new job as a traffic controller.

programme.title: Counting With Paula
programme.sub-title: Hocus Pocus / The Greatest Show on Treasure Island
programme.desc: All of Tim's magic tricks are failing. It must be because his magic wand is spoilt!

programme.title: ducktv
programme.desc: The learn with ducktv section presents ducktv shows with a strong educational aspect. The episodes of this category teach first concepts, music and art, stimulate imagination and creativity, and develop cognitive skills.

programme.title: ducktv
programme.desc: In each season ducktv presents special seasonal episodes from diverse series. The shows of the seasons on ducktv section promote seasonal activities, reinforces the identification of seasonal characteristics and teach about the cycles of nature.

programme.title: Super Mario Bros 3
programme.desc: Super Mario Bros 3 - Do the Koopa / Kootie Pie Rocks: Luigi finds a map to the Doom Dancer music box which Mario and friends have to find it in Dark Land which can stop King Koopa and his Koopalings ruling the Mushroom Kingdom. / The Marios and Toad accompany the Princess to a Milli Vinilli concert in New York City, but Kottie Pie, jealous that's she's not going to the concert, kidnaps Rob and Fab for her own.

programme.title: Super Mario Bros 3
programme.desc: Super Mario Bros 3 - Crimes R Us / Life's Ruff: King Koopa busts out a top criminal so he can teach the Koopalings to steal and to stop the Mario Brothers. / Hip and Hop Koopa turn Luigi and King Whim-bag, of the ice world, into dogs with the king's wand.

programme.title: Pac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures
programme.desc: Pac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures - Planet Pac: Dr. Buttocks lures a giant Ghosteroidto threaten PacWorld, and Pac-Man must grow to planet proportions to save the day.

programme.title: Pac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures
programme.desc: Pac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures - Stand by Your Pac-Man: It's ValenPac Day and Pinky is so jealous of Pac's friendship with Cyli, she tries to get rid of her rival, which winds up getting Pac captured by the Pacinator... who is responsible for 'icing' the Last Yellow Ones!

programme.title: Skylanders Academy
programme.desc: Skylanders Academy - Dream Girls: Stealth Elf has her mind manipulated by new villain Dreamcatcher.

programme.title: Skylanders Academy
programme.desc: Skylanders Academy - The Hole Truth: The young Skylanders serve a day of guard duty at Cloudcracker Prison.

programme.title: Street Sharks
programme.desc: Street Sharks - Shark Treatment: Dr. Paradigm uses mind control to turn Jets Taylor into Moby Lick, a whale mutant and his newest Seaviate.

programme.title: Street Sharks
programme.desc: Street Sharks - Road Sharks: After Streex is captured by Dr. Paradigm, the Sharks and Moby Lick set out to rescue him.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh!
programme.desc: Yu-Gi-Oh! - Awakening of Evil Part 4: Odion uses a phony Egyptian God Card to summon the third Egyptian God Monster, The Winged Dragon of Ra.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh!
programme.desc: Yu-Gi-Oh! - Mind Game Part 1: Mai vs. Marik: The third duel of the Battle City finals between Mai and Marik is no ordinary duel it's a battle where losing Life Points equals losing your memories.

programme.title: Superhero Kindergarten
programme.desc: Superhero Kindergarten - Austrian Adventure: The Super Ks take a trip to Austria with Mr. Arnold to solve the mystery of the missing mini horse.

programme.title: Superhero Kindergarten
programme.desc: Superhero Kindergarten - Emerson: A new exchange student from England teaches the Super Ks that appreciating each other's differences can save the day.

programme.title: Talking Tom and Friends
programme.desc: Friendship goes bad when Talking Tom's happy customer service representative turns into a villain.

programme.title: Legendary Places, Ninjago's Most Wanted
programme.desc: Cole joins Jay and Zane inside the Nindroid to solve a mysterious puzzle and also Kai enters Zane's mind and suggests the Ninja take a break from puzzle-solving to figure out which villain is trying to hack their friend.

programme.title: Fish Story!
programme.desc: Vic and Owen find themselves, along with Sinjin, in the middle of a massive dino-fight in the old Jurassic Park Visitor Center.

programme.title: Now or Never Witch
programme.desc: As the team battles this deadly foe, the Never Witch begins her ultimate spell, one that could unravel the very fabric of the dream world.

programme.title: Never Forever
programme.desc: With the dream world on the brink of collapse, the dream chasers launch a risky plan to defeat the Never Witch

programme.title: Sheep Freak
programme.desc: The dream chasers manage a challenging incursion by returning a doom sheep to the dream world, where they discover a dark force increasing in power.

programme.title: Holiday Gift Swap Disaster
programme.desc: While the rest of the group gets creative and makes new, personalized gifts from scratch. In the end, it's a reminder that it's not about the gifts—it's about the thought and effort you put into showing you care.

programme.title: Day Of The Great Devourer
programme.desc: Disaster threatens as the ninja must fight to defeat the all consuming Great Devourer in a desperate attempt to save Ninjago.

programme.title: LEGO Marvel Super Heroes
programme.desc: The Avengers prepare for a party with a surprise guest when Ultron crashes the preparations by using mind control on Iron Man. While trying to rescue their leader, the rest of the team discovers that Baron Stucker, HYDRA, and Yellowjacket are in on Ultron's plans. While preparing for a party at Avengers' Tower, THE AVENGERS (IRON MAN, CAPTAIN AMERICA, BLACK WIDOW, HAWKEYE, THOR, HULK and VISION) notice Iron Man acting strangely. When Iron Man volunteers to go out and get more ice, they find it odd: when has Tony every volunteered to get anything? Suddenly, ULTRON appears on a screen: he's taken control of Iron Man! To make matters worse, Ultron takes over all the versions of Iron Man's armor and they fly off! Finally, the Avengers confront Ultron and his Ultron Minions as they attack Avengers' Tower. Ultron's ultimate plan: use all the data/stuff they've stolen to free the newly rebuilt IRON LEGION, destroy the Avengers, and control the world. Tony dons the hastily repaired Hulk Buster Armor and it's on! It's a huge battle as the Avengers try to save their beloved HQ and defeat Ultron. Will they succeed? And, more importantly, can their party be saved after Ultron tries to take down their Tower?The Avengers must now fight one of their own, and they're not sure what to do! Cap and Thor lead the charge, but can't fend off Iron Man and Ultron's Minions. Meanwhile, Black Widow, Hawkeye and Hulk track down a signal that seems to be controlling Iron Man from a castle in the “Little Sokovia” section of New York. There they fight HYDRA and BARON VON STRUCKER and learn that YELLOWJACKET is the “bug” that infected Iron Man's suit for Ultron. To help break Iron Man free from Ultron's control, the Avengers call in their own bug...ANT MAN!

programme.title: The Book Of Obsession
programme.desc: The Knights are tasked with protecting the King's valuable Ned Knights comic book during the Bookingheim Knight-a-Con. But with all the costumed kids there, will they recognize the real Jestro and Book of Monsters coming to get them? King Halbert instructs the Knights that they must take his valuable Ned Knights comic book to the “Knight-a-Kon” in Bookingham. It will be the first be first prize at the Knight-a-Kon. But the Book of Monsters wants the book, too. Having devoured Ned Knightly comic books in it's down time it has become fanatically obsessed with the storyline. General Magmar suggests they simply attack the place and take what they want, but Jestro tells them they can't: everyone will be in costume and they'll be thousands of comics there. This calls for a clever “infiltration” mission (which will also allow Jestro to see the Kon, which he's always wanted to do.) So Jestro goes into the Kon with the BOM and some monsters. And it's easy because everyone is in costume Jestro, the BOM and their monsters fit right in at the convention and Clay and crew have their work cut out for them trying to separate fiend from fan. Macy keeps going on-and-on about the story in the King's comic book as the Knights keep a watchful eye on it. Robin even shows up in the “Minitrex” – a small version of the Fortrex he built just for the Knight-a-Kon. He's convinced he can win the costume competition that is the big closing event for the Kon. At the end of the Kon there's a big awards ceremony for “Best Costume.” The prize: “Ned Knightly, Knight of Knighton, Issue #1.” The BOM must get it and scheme to get closer to it during the costume contest. He's just…about…to get the book when…the impatient Magmar attacks! Everyone thinks it's part of the big show! “Ned Knightly, Knight of Knighton, Issue #1” is tossed from person-to-person-to-person in the melee. The Knights must do something, but they're too far away from the Fortrex to download any NEXO-Power. Robin pulls up in his Minitrex they can use it to get NEXO-Power! Robin wins the best costume for his Minitrex and wins the Ned Knightly Comic which he then gives to Macy…she deserves to read it.

programme.title: Crocodile Tears

programme.title: The Monsters and the Mech!

programme.title: The Snowball Effect

programme.title: Ley-La, Rise of a Music Star

programme.title: Rise Of The Snakes

programme.title: LEGO Marvel Super Heroes

programme.title: The Book Of Monsters, Part 1
programme.desc: When Jestro the Jester finds the Book of Monsters, chaos breaks loose in the Kingdom of Knighton. Can the newly graduated Knights from the Knights' Academy save the kingdom? It's final graduation fighting exams for the latest Knights from Knights' Academy. All our new Knights demonstrate their prowess: CLAY, AARON, AXL and LANCE. PRINCESS MACY get to join them, but her father doesn't want her to graduate. As part of the entertainment, MERLOK does an amazing magic trick. His act is followed by…JESTRO, the King's Jester. He's terrible (and he should know to never follow a better act.) Everyone laughed because he's a real joke....a total failure. While Jestro roams the halls fo the King's Castle, forlorn, he hears the BOOK OF MONSTERS calling to him from Merlok's library. The book talks Jestro into gathering as many books of spells as he can carry, but they're interrupted by...Clay! As Jestro quickly pages through the Book of Monsters to attack Clay, Merlok arrives. There's a battle in the library between Jestro and Merlok. Finally, the wizard summons up his most powerful spell in self-defence. BOOM! There's a big, magical explosion and when Clay forces his way back into the library, Merlok, Jestro and the magic books are...GONE!

programme.title: Buddy Rich, Tom Warrington, Bob Kaye & John Marshall
programme.sub-title: Buddy Rich Big Band - The North Sea Jazz
programme.desc: The North Sea Jazz Festival is the largest indoor music festival in the world, known globally as the event where the past, present and future of jazz are featured within three days. Next to a firm base of jazz as the festival's staple music genre, many others, such as blues, soul, funk, or hip hop, pass by. Buddy Rich, one of the most influential jazz drummers of all time, performed at the North Sea Jazz Festival of 1978 with his orchestra. Rich's drumming was known for its remarkable speed and dexterity, which he achieved without any formal education.

programme.title: Junior Mance, Geoff Keezer, Bill Charlap & Alex Wilson
programme.desc: When renowned jazz pianists of the old and new school meet at a "Solo Piano Summit," audiences are treated to many an interesting musical surprise. The "Solo Piano Summit" brings together a handful of top pianists with different musical backgrounds: Jacky Terrasson, Junior Mance, Geoff Keezer, Bill Charlap and Alex Wilson. Besides solo performances the all-stars used the occasion for spontaneous interplay, improvisation and a fantastic finale - in short for the pleasure of virtuoso jazz piano playing.

programme.title: Charles Lloyd
programme.desc: An extraordinary collection of clips in all jazz genres from the most beautiful concerts and the most special festivals, performed by the finest jazz musicians.

programme.title: Ahmad Jamal Trio
programme.sub-title: Ahmad Jamal Trio - North Sea Jazz
programme.desc: The North Sea Jazz Festival is the largest indoor music festival in the world, known globally as the event where the past, present and future of jazz are featured within three days. Next to a firm base of jazz as the festival's staple music genre, many others, such as blues, soul, funk, or hip hop, pass by. In 1989, legendary jazz pianist Ahmad Jamal brought his trio to the North Sea Jazz Festival. Accompanied by James Cammack on bass and David Bowler on drums, Jamal showcased his excellence.

programme.title: Charles Lloyd
programme.desc: An extraordinary collection of clips in all jazz genres from the most beautiful concerts and the most special festivals, performed by the finest jazz musicians.

programme.title: Jon Hendricks, Judith Hendricks, Kim Lindsay & Mart Ledford
programme.desc: Since 1977, Estival is a summer jazz festival in Switzerland, Lugano. Estival offers a thrilling and particularly surprising line-up that explores the rich world of contemporary music whilst promoting the understanding of different cultures, tolerance, and co-existence. John Carl Hendricks is one of the instigators of "vocalese", an art form that aims to add lyrics to existing instrumental pieces and replace several instruments with singing. He flaunts this technique at Estival, where he "scat" while playing double bass. Hendricks is known for his mastery of scat, a form of vocal jazz that uses onomatopoeia rather than lyrics

programme.title: Robert Glasper & Jason Moran
programme.sub-title: La Gaité Lyrique: Robert Glasper & Jason Moran
programme.desc: As part of the Blue Note Jazz Festival and Blue Note Records' 75th anniversary, pianists Robert Glasper and Jason Moran delivered a stunning jazz piano duet, resulting in an exciting exploration of the great history of jazz. An intricate knowledge of African-American music is infused with modern energy. The two musicians reinterpret the stride piano tradition through captivating original songs and personal interpretations of the music of Albert Ammons, Andrew Hill, Thelonious Monk, Ornette Coleman and Herbie Hancock.

programme.title: Leonard Slatkin, Orchestre National de Lyon, Olga Kern & Sol Gabetta
programme.desc: American conductor Leonard Slatkin leads the Orchestre National de Lyon in a concert recorded at the Auditorium de Lyon in 2014. The concert opens with William Bolcom´s Circus Ouverture, which was specially composed for Slatkin's 70th birthday. The concert continues with a performance of Beethoven's Triple Concerto in C, Op. 56 performed by three female soloists: Olga Kern (piano), Baibe Skride (violin) and Sol Gabetta (cello). The work is the only concerto which Beethoven ever completed for more than one solo instrument. The concert closes with Ravel's beloved orchestration of Mussorgsky's Pictures at an Exhibition.

programme.title: Seong-Jin Cho
programme.desc: Cinematic classical clips and energizing concert pieces featuring up-and-coming artists.

programme.title: Margaret Price, Orchester der Ludwigsburger Schlossfestspiele & Wolfgang Gönnenwein
programme.desc: Welsh soprano Margaret Price performs Gustav Mahler's Rückert-Lieder. She is accompanied by the Orchester der Ludwigsburger Schloßfestspiele under the baton of Wolfgang Gönnenwein. Mahler's Rückert-Lieder are a collection of five songs based on poetry of Friedrich Rückert (1788-1866). Mahler completed both a piano and an orchestral version of the first four songs during the summer of 1901, but he never got around to orchestrating the fifth song, “Liebst du um Schönheit” (‘Lovest thou but beauty'). Mahler wrote this overt love song for his bride Alma Schindler. This performance was recorded at the 1988 Ludwigsburger Schloßfestspiele.

programme.title: Daniel Rowland
programme.sub-title: Strauss - Piano Quartet in C minor, Op. 13
programme.desc: Violinist Daniel Rowland, violist Rachel Roberts, cellist Julian Arp, and pianist Diana Ketler perform Richard Strauss's Piano Quartet in C minor, Op. 13, as part of the Stiftfestival 2016 in the Dutch village of Weerselo. In his early years, Strauss tried to compose different types of chamber music works, including a piano quartet. The young composer started writing the Piano Quartet in C minor in the spring of 1884 and completed it one year later. The work is in four movements and shows considerable influence from Johannes Brahms. It premiered on December 8, 1885 in Weimar and was performed by members of the Halír Quartet with Strauss himself playing the piano part. The following year the work won the first prize given by the Berliner Tonkünstlerverein for a piano quartet. This performance was recorded at the Stiftkerk in Weerselo, the Netherlands.

programme.title: Daniel Rowland
programme.sub-title: Strauss - Piano Quartet in C minor, Op. 13 - I.
programme.desc: Violinist Daniel Rowland, violist Rachel Roberts, cellist Julian Arp, and pianist Diana Ketler perform Richard Strauss's Piano Quartet in C minor, Op. 13, as part of the Stiftfestival 2016 in the Dutch village of Weerselo. In his early years, Strauss tried to compose different types of chamber music works, including a piano quartet. The young composer started writing the Piano Quartet in C minor in the spring of 1884 and completed it one year later. The work is in four movements and shows considerable influence from Johannes Brahms. It premiered on December 8, 1885 in Weimar and was performed by members of the Halír Quartet with Strauss himself playing the piano part. The following year the work won the first prize given by the Berliner Tonkünstlerverein for a piano quartet. This performance was recorded at the Stiftkerk in Weerselo, the Netherlands.

programme.title: Daniel Rowland
programme.desc: Cinematic classical clips and energizing concert pieces featuring up-and-coming artists.

programme.title: Ballet of the Mariinsky Theatre
programme.desc: 'La Bayadère' is a three-act ballet by the French choreographer Marius Petipa. Set in 19th-century India, 'La Bayadère' tells the sad love story of the noble warrior Solor and his Nikia, a temple dancer or 'bayadère', who is poisoned by her rival, Gamzatti. The ballet features one of the most celebrated scenes of classical ballet, namely 'The Kingdom of the Shades'. In this iconic scene, Solor dreams, under the influence of opium, of reuniting with his beloved Nikia. This production of the ballet features the revised choreography by Vladimir Ponomarev and Vakhtang Chabukiani (1941), and includes dances by Konstantin Sergeyev and Nikolai Zubkovsky. The ballet's music, composed by Ludwig Minkus, is performed by the Mariinsky Orchestra under the direction of Boris Gruzin. Among the performers are Viktoria Tereshkina, Anastasia Matvienko, Vladimir Shklyarov, and Vladimir Ponomarev. This performance was recorded at the Mariinsky Theatre in St. Petersburg, Russia, in July 2014.

programme.title: Nikolay Lugansky & Vadim Rudenko
programme.sub-title: Piano Duets - Mozart, Ravel, Rachmaninoff a. o.
programme.desc: In this splendid concert, exceptional Russian pianists Nikolay Lugansky and Vadim Rudenko join forces in a program for two pianos comprising works of Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart, Maurice Ravel, Anton Arensky, Sergei Rachmaninoff, and Nikolai Kapustin. Both pianists, laureates of the prestigious International Tchaikovsky Competition in 1994, have received international recognition. They perform regularly in a duo. On the program: Mozart's Sonata for two pianos in D major, K. 448/375 A Ravel's La Valse (arr. for two pianos) Arensky's Suite No. 1 for two pianos, Op. 15 and Rachmaninoff's Suite No. 1 (Fantasy) for two pianos, Op. 5. As an encore, the duo performs ‘Romance' from Rachmaninoff's Suite No. 2 for two pianos, Op. 17, followed by ‘Overture' from Nikolai Kapustin's Sinfonietta, Op. 49. This performance was recorded at the Tchaikovsky Concert Hall in Moscow, Russia, on November 25, 2021.

programme.title: Paul Simon
programme.desc: During the first leg of Paul Simon's triumphant, 2011 U.S. tour, he thrilled hometown fans with a special club performance at New York City's historic Webster Hall. The show presents this iconic artist working at the peak of his artistic and creative powers in an unusually intimate setting. The 20-song, 90-minute set list was drawn from his legendary career and includes several songs that haven't been performed live in many years. "Kodachrome," "Mother and Child Reunion," "Gone At Last" and "The Obvious Child" are just some of the highlights as well as songs from Simon's most recent album, 2011's So Beautiful or So What (Hear Music/Concord Music Group) including "Dazzling Blue," "Rewrite," "The Afterlife" and the album's propulsive title track.
